other.presetdesolatedsc: Sendiri dalam kegelapan. Sedikit yang bisa dijumpai dengan bahaya dimana-mana. Meski penyitas jarang dijumpai, yang kamu jumpai biasanya memiliki banyak yang untuk dibagi.
other.presetunchippeddsc: Meski ketiadaan chip meringankan tuntutan batas waktu yang membatasi lainnya, itu juga membuat mendiagnosis dirimu jauh lebih sulit. Mungkin itu akan sepadan di akhirnya?
other.presetendlessdsc:
Tak ada jalan keluar; inilah hukuman matimu. Seberapa jauh kamu bisa capai, dengan bahaya yang terus bertambah?

[CATATAN: Tidak tersedia untuk sekarang, karena game sudah tanpa akhir bagaimanapun]
other.runsettingsdsc: Pilihlah gaya main dari prasetel di bawah. Kamu bisa ubah mereka di panel pengaturan di samping.
other.runsetunchippeddsc: Menonaktifkan chip kesehatanmu, memaksamu untuk lebih berusaha dalam mencari tahu penyakit yang kamu derita. Mengaktifkan garis pandang.
other.runsetbaselootdensitydsc: Seberapa banyak jarahan yang muncul di setiap lapisan. Mempengaruhi hal-hal seperti kapsul turunan, kapsul penyelamat, peti
other.runsetlootmultiplierdsc:
Kepadatan jarahan dikalikan dengan ini untuk setiap lapisan yang kamu lalui

dengan kata lain, jika kamu menyetelnya ke 0,5x contohnya, maka jumlah jarahan akan hilang setengah setiap kali kamu turun ke lapisan lainnya
other.runsetbasetrapdensitydsc: Seberapa banyak jebakan yang muncul di setiap lapisan. Mempengaruhi hal-hal seperti jebakan mekanikal, musuh, bahaya lainnya.
other.runsettrapincreasedsc:
Angka ini ditambahkan ke kepadatan jebakan untuk setiap lapisan yang kamu lalui

dengan kata lain, jika kamu menyetelnya ke 0,5x contohnya, maka jumlah jebakan akan berubah dari 1x -> 1,5x -> 2x dan seterusnya
other.runsettimelimitdsc: Dalam menit, seberapa lama kamu bisa menetap di setiap lapisan sebelum dihukum
other.runsetstartingsuppliesdsc: Barang-barang apa saja yang kamu akan bawa
other.runsetxpgaindsc: Pengali banyak skill pengalaman yang kamu dapat
other.runsetmetabolismratedsc: Seberapa cepat lapar/haus/mual/berat badan kamu terkuras.
other.runsethealingratedsc: Seberapa cepat luka sembuh. Mempengaruhi regenerasi otot/kulit, pembekuan darah, regenerasi darah, patah tulang, terkilir dan kerusakan otak
other.runsetfracturepaindsc: Seberapa sakit anggota tubuhmu ketika digunakan saat terkilir/patah tulang
other.runsetbleedratedsc: Seberapa cepat darahmu terkuras dari luka berdarah
other.runsetinfectionspeeddsc: Seberapa cepat infeksi bertambah parah/mereda dan seberapa cepat itu membunuh, dan juga mempengaruhi seberapa lama antiseptik bertahan
other.runsetinfectionchancedsc: Seberapa mungkin dirimu terjangkit infeksi dari luka
other.runsetfibrillationratedsc: Seberapa cepat irama fibrasi jantung berkembang. Angka yang besar = mati lebih cepat
other.runsetbonuslimbarmordsc: Seberapa besar kerusakan yang dapat diredam oleh bagian tubuhmu. Nilai 1 akan membagi setengah kerusakan yang datang
other.runsetstaminaregendsc: Seberapa cepat staminamu pulih setelah digunakan
other.runsetattackdamagedsc: Seberapa besar kerusakan yang bisa di keluarkan oleh ayunanmu
other.runsetstrokesdsc: Menentukan jika kamu bisa stroke saat krisis hipertensi
other.runsetbraindamagefxdsc: Menentukan jika kerusakan otak akan menyebabkan gejolak berkala
other.runsetinfinitelaststanddsc: Menentukan jika kamu bisa memiliki lebih dari satu kali kesempatan untuk mendapati pijakan akhir dalam permainan
other.runsetmoodnormalizationratedsc: Seberapa cepat emosimu kembali ke 0/ Nilai yang lebih tinggi berarti lebih susah untuk tetap bahagia ataupun sedih.
other.runsetforcesleepdsc: Haruskah pemain dipaksa tidak sadarkan diri ketika energi mereka sampai 0
other.runsetlowmoodeventsdsc: Haruskah pemain mengambil tindakan khusus ketika dalam emosi buruk yang kritis

other.tutbasic1: Selamat datang di kursus pelatihan revisi terakhir.
other.tutbasic2: Kita akan pelajari semua hal dasar yang kamu perlu ketahui untuk eksperimen berikutnya yang akan kamu ikuti.
other.tutbasic3: Silakan jalan menuju Perangkat Tangan Latihan. [A]
other.tutbasic6: [SPACE] untuk lompat. Tahan untuk melompat lebih tinggi. Kamu juga bisa melompat dari dinding.
other.tutbasic7: Coba mencapai ke Perangkat Tangan Latihan.
other.tutbasic10: [KLIK KANAN] untuk interaksi barang. Klik dirimu untuk melihat inventarismu, klik di luar untuk menutup.
other.tutbasic11: Ini sesuatu untuk kamu mainkan. [RIGHT CLICK] dan tarik ke dirimu, lalu tarik ke salah satu slot invetarismu. Tahan [CONTROL] untuk melihat label benda, biar lebih mudah melihatnya.
other.tutbasic12: Bagus. Kamu juga bisa gunakan benda dari inventaris. Tarik benda ke tengah lingkaran.
other.tutbasic13: Luar biasa gak rasanya? Silakan habiskan makanannya.
other.tutbasic13fail: Kamu seharusnya mengambilnya, bukan langsung dimakan... Ya ampun, sampai habis pula..?
other.tutbasic15: Mari kita coba sesuatu yang lain. Ini benda lain yang bisa kamu mainkan.
other.tutbasic16: Kamu punya enam slot utama untuk menaruh benda. Dua tangan, satu mulut, tiga slot di punggungmu.
other.tutbasic17: Pegang ini di salah satu tanganmu.
other.tutbasic18: Alat-alat tertentu bisa digunakan dari "Tangan utama" kamu. Taruh garu di slot itu dan coba ayukan dengan [KLIK KIRI].
other.tutbasic19: Kamu berkelahi dengan hantu sekarang... Bagus!
other.tutbasic20: Kamu juga bisa cepat menukar apapun di tanganmu dengan [Q]. Cobalah!
other.tutbasic21: Kamu juga bisa melempar barang. Tahan [T] lalu lepaskan untuk melempar apapun yang ada di tangan utamamu. Bidik ke target yang kami baru buat!
other.tutbasic23: Taruh garu itu ke tanganmu lagi.
other.tutbasic24: Kamu bisa menggali benda-benda dengan mengayun. Ini akan membuatmu cepat lelah, tapi berguna jika dilakukan sesekali.
other.tutbasic25: Gali ke sisi lain. Tahan [S] untuk berjongkok.
other.tutbasic26: Beberapa kondisi seperti rasa sakit atau kelelahan butuh waktu untuk pulih. Percepat waktu [1-3] untuk memulih lebih cepat.
other.tutbasic28: Sekarang, kita akan pelajari dasar-dasar pertolongan pertama. Mohon tetap diam.
other.tutbasic29: Aduh! Lenganmu sakit dan berdarah! Mari kita periksa. Buka panel kesehatan dengan [R] atau tombol hati.
other.tutbasic30: Arahkan kursor ke anggota tubuh yang sakit untuk melihat info lanjut.
other.tutbasic31: Luka tusuk yang cukup besar. Kabar baik - kamu akan memperbaikinya!
other.tutbasic32: Here's some dressing. Mouse over it while holding [SHIFT]. It says "Usable in health panel".
other.tutbasic33: Pick it up, then open the health panel.
other.tutbasic34: [RIGHT CLICK] drag the dressing over the damaged limb.
other.tutbasic35: Time for some physical work. [LEFT CLICK] wrap the dressing around! For testing purposes, it's infinite.
other.tutbasic36: You're saved! For now, at least. You should always have some dressings on you. They save lives.
other.tutbasic37: Shucks. Looks like that wound got infected! (Normally you'd only find out a while later.)
other.tutbasic38: Another important medicine! Some antiseptic, read what it does if you wish. Pick it up and open the health panel.
other.tutbasic39: Same story as before. Apply the antiseptic to the infected site.
other.tutbasicfail1: You... Used the entire thing. In the wrong spot...
other.tutbasicfail2: Lets just... Imagine you did it correctly.
other.tutbasic40: Neat! Do this every few minutes to make the infection disappear. The arrow on the icon shows if it's regressing or progressing.
other.tutbasic41: You really don't want to ignore infections; They are lethal if left to progress. Being well fed and in good shape will slow them down.
other.tutbasic42: More detailed health info is provided in the First Aid Course. You can do it later if you wish.
other.tutbasic44: Trash bag! You should be very familiar with it. We've seen your kind sleeping in them sometimes.
other.tutbasic45: It's a container, meaning it extends how many things you can have on you. Try dragging another item into it.
other.tutbasic46: Wow, it's stored inside! Now [RIGHT CLICK] the bag and pull the item back out.
other.tutbasic46fail1: Wh... Why... Why'd you rip it up..?
other.tutbasic46fail3: You are testing my patience. Stop ripping the bags apart. We will skip this segment as you are not cooperating.
other.tutbasic47: Well done. Containers are extremely useful to have, keep an eye out for any.
other.tutbasic48: Here's something new. See if you can figure out how to put it on.
other.tutbasic49: Nice! Wearables work outside of the normal inventory slots. This one can also have stuff put in it!
other.tutbasic50: Try putting the empty trash bag into the backpack you're wearing. You can put empty containers inside others!
other.tutbasic51: Procuring things on your own is a very useful skill.
other.tutbasic52: Open the crafting menu by pressing [C] (or the button).
other.tutbasic53: You'll make a foliage bag. To do that, you'll need to make two canvases and one rope. We have given you some foliage to do so.
other.tutbasic54: Recipes you can make are at the top of the list highlighted in white. Click on the rope recipe, then on the rope icon in the top right.
other.tutbasic55: Nice. Halfway there. Make the canvases now.
other.tutbasic56: When you have both canvases and the rope on you, make the foliage bag.
other.tutbasic57: Well done! You can also switch between recipe categories, or use the search bar. You'll get more info in the Crafting Course.
other.tutbasic58: Lets take a quick look at the health panel again.
other.tutbasic59: Look up in the top right. Those are your skills. In order, Strength, Resilience and Intelligence.
other.tutbasic60: Strength is your amount of muscle mass. It mainly improves your attacks and carrying capacity.
other.tutbasic61: Resilience is your physical endurance and mental fortitude. It makes you more resistant to pain and tiredness.
other.tutbasic62: Intelligence defines your knowledge. It affects what you can craft, recognize, trader interactions and defibrillator usage.
other.tutbasic63: You can hover over either of the skills to learn more.
other.tutbasic64: Strength and Resilience can be additionally increased by working out. Try it, using the button in the lower right.
other.tutbasic65: You might've also noticed the nap button right under exercise.
other.tutbasic66: Sleep is important for recovering your energy. We made you a little bed to sleep on, try napping there!
other.tutbasic67: The surface on which you sleep matters! Rubber is especially comfy, while rocks are particularly harsh. It's good to give your back a break sometimes.
other.tutbasic68: This concludes your final revisional training. You may now return to your designated room.
other.tutaid1: Welcome to the First Aid Course. We will go over your body, some injuries and how to treat them.
other.tutaid2: Open the health panel. You can bark at me to skip through this part.
other.tutaid3: It's time to start paying attention to the big panel to the left. It might be overwhelming for a while, but we'll touch on the more important things.
other.tutaid4: This is your brain integrity. When your brain dies, you die. Today you'll learn how to keep IT alive.
other.tutaid5: This is your consciousness, or how aware of your surroundings you are.
other.tutaid6: This is your total pain. It usually goes down by itself over time.
other.tutaid7: This is your blood volume. It will go down as you bleed it out, eventually leading to death. Consider 2.5L to be around the lethal limit.
other.tutaid8: This is your rate of bleeding. Fortunately, you're not bleeding right now, so there's nothing displayed.
other.tutaid9: This is your immunity, a metric of how capable your immune system is at defending your body from infection.
other.tutaid10: This is your stamina. Physical exertion will lower it.
other.tutaid11: This is your respiratory rate. Your oxygen will lower if you don't breathe enough.
other.tutaid12: This is your absorbed radiation. Don't ask what that is, just know that more than zero means trouble.
other.tutaid13: This is your heartrate. You'll want to keep this above 0.
other.tutaid14: This is your blood pressure. Don't let it get too high or too low.
other.tutaid15: This is your blood oxygen, also referred to as SpO2.
other.tutaid16: This is an ECG. It visualizes your heart rhythm. Get used to it, as you'll stare at it often.
other.tutaid17: Lastly, this is the limb-specific health panel. Most of it is pretty self explanatory, so we won't cover it for now.
other.tutaid18: ...It's a lot, but you can just check your moodles at any time to learn most of these. Don't worry about it too much.
other.tutaid19: We will now get to some common injuries.
other.tutaid20: Bleeding is likely the most common thing you will encounter. It risks infection, and more importantly, lowers your total blood volume.
other.tutaid21: Your brain needs oxygen to live. To get oxygen, it needs a sufficient amount of flowing, oxygenated blood.
other.tutaid22: Here's some dressing. You know how to use it, fix your bleeding.
other.tutaid23: Take a look at your blood pressure. Normally it should stay around 120/80.
other.tutaid24: Blood pressure is directly affected by your heart rate, blood volume and blood vessel constriction/dilation.
other.tutaid25: In crisis scenarios, survival boils down to making sure your blood pressure stays at a safe level and that your heart keeps beating.
other.tutaid26: I am now removing your blood. Keep an eye on your blood pressure.
other.tutaid27: Your blood pressure is now really low, forcing your heart to compensate by speeding up.
other.tutaid28: This only goes so far, though. With really high heart rates or really low pressures, something called FIBRILLATION will start.
other.tutaid29: Fibrillation is the process of your heart rhythm getting more erratic until it stops. It kills in about two minutes.
other.tutaid30: You might notice an arrhythmia moodle in the bottom left. This is a sign of said fibrillation.
other.tutaid31: This is an Automated External Defibrillator. It resets your fibrillation to zero, stabilizing you for a while.
other.tutaid32: Use it on your chest and follow the steps it gives you. Good luck.
other.tutaid34: Now, I want you to notice something. Look at your ECG.
other.tutaid35: We will sweep your heart through all stages of fibrillation. I want you to look at how the rhythm changes with it.
other.tutaid36: Do you see how it becomes distinctly wavey at high fibrillation?
other.tutaid37: This is a manual defibrillator. It's similar to the AED, but can fail if used wrong. Use it on your chest.
other.tutaidfail1: Wrong spot. Use it on your CHEST.
other.tutaidfail2: Chest. Your thorax. You used it in the wrong spot... Use it on your CHEST. You want to defibrillate your heart... Which is located in your chest. How did you even get to this point?
other.tutaid38: To use it correctly, you must match the power level to how far fibrillation has progressed.
other.tutaid39: This means that if arrhythmia just started, you set the power level low. If your heart is seconds from stopping, you set it high.
other.tutaid40: You can determine this using the ECG and power estimation visible here. The better you align the power level, the higher chance of it working.
other.tutaid41: Try doing that. Set power, charge then shock until your arrhythmia goes away. You'll get it eventually. Try using the ECG and power estimation.
other.tutaid42: Lets say something goes horribly wrong. Wear this, then open the health panel.
other.tutaid43: Your heart is no longer beating. The only thing keeping you conscious is the autopump you just put on. This is the worst case scenario.
other.tutaid44: Defibrillators can fix this, but it'll take a while. There's a better way.
other.tutaid45: Epinephrine is a special drug that can restart your heart, found in combat pens. Inject it.
other.tutaid47: Theres something notable that isn't shown in the health panel, that being blood viscosity.
other.tutaid48: Blood viscosity is simply how thick or diluted your blood is. Both extremes can cause issues, but extremely thick blood is more dangerous.
other.tutaid49: Extremely thick blood can cause a Pulmonary Embolism, which will damage your thorax. Severe damage to the thorax will impair and eventually stop your breathing.
other.tutaid50: To break the clot down, use streptokinase.
other.tutaid51: Nicely done, but lets see what happens if your thorax did fail.
other.tutaid52: You must increase your thorax muscle health to breathe again. This bruise kit does just that - use it on your thorax.
other.tutaid53: Back in working order. Low respiration will lower your SpO2, which will eventually cause unconsciousness, fibrillation and death.
other.tutaid54: One more thing to note about circulation is high blood pressure, or hypertension. It's usually more manageable, but can still be very dangerous.
other.tutaid55: The main danger is the risk of stroke if hypertension becomes too severe. Try not to let your blood pressure go above 180/120.
other.tutaid56: You'll feel a lot of this out with experience. You will now be given some supplies. Bark at me with [B] when you're ready to move on.
other.tutaid57: We will now take a look at some more miscellaneous injuries.
other.tutaid58: Dislocations are a common injury, usually acquired from bad falls. Dislocated limbs cause a lot of pain when used.
other.tutaid59: You can fix dislocations by hand. Try it.
other.tutaid60: You need to hit the joint back into place. Gain some speed with your hand, then [LEFT CLICK] over the joint to hit it hard.
other.tutaid61: Fractures are similar to dislocations, but are more severe and need either time or special tools to fix.
other.tutaid62: A splint is a very simple, yet effective way to treat a fracture. Put this splint on your broken hand.
other.tutaid63: Neat. Splints speed up the healing of a fractured limb significantly. Putting dressing on the limb would also help slightly.
other.tutaid64: Your limbs stop functioning when their muscle health nears zero, making it so you, for example, can't hold things with the respective limb.
other.tutaid65: You can switch which hand you use as your main in the bottom right of the health panel. Try it. Note that you're not ambidextrous.
other.tutaid66: Severe pain is one of the most debilitating ailments you can get.
other.tutaid67: It immobilizes you, quickly worsens mood and can make you pass out from shock.
other.tutaid68: Luckily for you, there's a pretty simple way to treat it.
other.tutaid69: Opiates quickly reduce pain across your entire body when used. Inject the entire thing and see.
other.tutaid72: Be careful with opiates - you can overdose on them easily, or go into withdrawals after. Naloxone and naltrexone can help with opiate abuse.
other.tutaid73: This concludes the First Aid Course. We could cover more, but we believe you can safely figure out other afflictions. You may return to your assigned room.
other.tutcraft1: Welcome to the Crafting Course. Today, we will cover the details of procuring items and show certain common workflows.
other.tutcraft2: Almost everything you see is real matter and not trash.
other.tutcraft3: You can harvest metal from scrap piles and traps, wood from wood structures, foliage from plants, etc.
other.tutcraft4: Your objective will be to create a specified item using the resources given to you.
other.tutcraft5: We will assume you know the bare basics of crafting. Create a foliage bag using the following materials. Use the reset button if you make a mistake.
other.tutcraft6: First steps. Nicely done. Getting a container should always be your first goal.
other.tutcraft7: An important thing to note is that you can use items near you on the ground for crafting. Bark at me with [B] once you've fully absorbed this information.
other.tutcraft8: Lets try again with the same resources. Make a full dressing this time. You can drag any item onto the crafting button to see what recipes it can be used in.
other.tutcraft9: Nicely done. Dressings are very cheap to make and are crucial for your survival. They should be your priority right after getting a container.
other.tutcraft10: Important note - you can pin a recipe to have it permanently highlighted in the corner, using the button in the bottom right of the crafting menu. Pin the recipe for a flimsy knife.
other.tutcraft11: Lets add some scrap and wood to the mix. Create a flimsy knife using these resources. You can merge items in the inventory.
other.tutcraft12: Nicely done. Something to note is that you can favourite items to prevent them from being used in crafting recipes. You'd do this for stuff like bags, to not rip them up by accident.
other.tutcraft13: Press [F] on the flimsy knife you just made to favourite it.
other.tutcraft14: Now, try making a sterilized dressing using these items. Remember you can rip certain bags and clothing items via crafting.
other.tutcraft15: Nice. You might've noticed we made your INT level go up for this test.
other.tutcraft16: Crafting ability is tied to your intelligence level. You might've noticed that every recipe has its own INT requirement.
other.tutcraft17: You gain INT whenever you use a blueprint or make a recipe for the first time.
other.tutcraft18: You can make recipes that are up to 3 INT higher than your current INT, with progressively worse results.
other.tutcraft19: 1 INT higher has a 50% chance of damaging the resulting item. 2 INT, 50% chance of fully wasting material, and at 3 INT, you also damage yourself slightly.
other.tutcraft20: You have enough INT to make a machete now. Do that.
other.tutcraft21: Clever little fella, aren't you. You can further upgrade your tools with, for example, titanium alloys. Making digging tools is useful too.
other.tutcraft22: Lets move onto food-related crafts. Most of them require a campfire.
other.tutcraft23: Boil the groundwater into clean water. You'll need to make a canteen to collect it, and a campfire to boil it.
other.tutcraft26: Well done. We will move on to your final crafting challenge now.
other.tutcraft27: Make a mini laser drill and a battery to put inside of it. This is your final objective for today.
other.tutcraft29: This concludes the Crafting Course. Hopefully you can put into practice the knowledge you have attained. You may return to your assigned room.
other.startlorenote:
Tahun 2XXX 
<>
Teknologi umat manusia telah berkembang secara signifikan. Hal-hal yang dulunya tampak mustahil kini menjadi hal yang lumrah.
<>
Di pusat semua itu terdapat satu perusahaan. Kemajuan mereka terlihat ajaib, namun mereka menjaga rahasia mereka dengan sangat ketat.
<>
Bertahun-tahun yang lalu, mereka telah menciptakan spesies baru: kombinasi DNA hewan dan manusia yang mereka sebut sebagai "Eksperimen".
<>
Eksperimen-eksperimen ini adalah subjek uji yang sempurna, cukup mirip dengan manusia, namun sepenuhnya milik mereka untuk diperlakukan sesuka hati. Mereka memeras subjek-subjek tersebut, memanipulasi biologi mereka lebih jauh lagi hanya demi memenuhi kebutuhan mereka, dan seiring berjalannya waktu, populasinya pun terus bertambah.

Tak satu pun dari mereka akan pernah melihat dunia luar.
<>
Kini tampaknya Bumi tidak lagi mampu menampung ambisi mereka. Dengan teknologi teleportasi yang baru mereka ciptakan, mereka mengarahkan pandangan ke sebuah planet kelabu yang penuh dengan kehidupan.
<>
Tak lama kemudian, berton-ton kargo dan sampah dikirimkan.
<>
Akibat kekeliruan kalibrasi, jumlah yang berakhir di sistem gua planet tersebut sama banyaknya dengan yang mendarat di permukaan, namun proyek itu berjalan dengan lancar.
<>
Hal itu terus berlangsung sampai pada sebuah kesalahan yang tidak dikenali menyebabkan sebuah perangkat teknologi penting terkirim jauh ke dalam gua.
<>
Tak lama kemudian, para ilmuwan menyusun sebuah rencana. Sebuah rencana dengan risiko yang minim bagi diri mereka sendiri.
<>
Peluang keberhasilannya rendah, namun persediaan umpan hidup mereka berlimpah.
<>
Kamu adalah salah satu spesimen yang malang.
Perlawanan bukanlah sebuah pilihan.

Humans do strange things. You should never trust them to be nice.
But you can trust them to keep you alive.<>
I broke my arm once, and they put it in a "cast." I did not like it. It was heavy and stopped my arm from moving at all. It seemed they made it worse on purpose, and they made me wear it for a long time.<>
They removed it one day, and my arm was fine.<>
The humans made it worse, so it could get better.<>

I think they did something similar to Jumper's head.
Jumper used to scream and cry a lot. One time they hurt themselves, and the humans took them away.<>
Jumper is back now. They don't cry anymore. They don't talk. They look sleepy all the time. Their head is like my arm used to be. I can't see any "casts" around it, and their behavior worries me, but at least they don't hurt themselves anymore. I hope the humans undo whatever they did once Jumper's head is fixed.<>

I miss them.

The vines spread around the area are surprisingly climbable. The thin ones less so, but you can use the large ones as natural ladders. Definitely saved me a few fractured legs.

Today I happened upon a door for which unlocking was contingent upon the insertion of some scrap. I did not possess any on my person; however, I was fortunately equipped with a laser-action drill, which made quick work of the surrounding walls. Inside, I obtained an electronic device on which some exquisite music had been stored. I listen to it from time to time.

The oil pools on this planet are definitely unnatural. Trying to understand their origin, I crawled through one of the empty pipes only to find a tough metallic screen that stopped me from progressing.
There was a sign behind it that said "Waste Disposal Source". I drilled through the pipe to go around the screen, but the pipe stopped abruptly. It has to be connected to something from our old home, but I don't understand how.

That sign keeps worrying me. Did the humans want to dispose of us too?

There seems to be a high amount of radioactive waste in this area. While that does make it difficult to navigate, we should keep in mind that we can take brief doses of ionizing radiation without too much issue, as long as the exposure doesn't last too long. I've heard we're already riddled with cancer.

Something is wrong with the environment here. In the air, I can feel it- those dilapidated fuel cells make my fur stand on end. My skin flakes when I pass them, and my body burns. Even when I can't see them, I can feel that uneasy sensation building in the air around them. I've had to use my rope spool and even claw through the rocks here to find routes around them, but even then, there are purple stones that seem to carry the same effect, just in different doses.

one of the others I found fell onto a bunch of those purple rocks. i managed to pull them away but both of our coats are shedding. i don't know what's happening.<>




i ate one of the popping fruits by a life pod… things stopped hurting. but when they bit into one they started throwing up blood just minutes later. they're not breathing. what was different? they looked the same. am i just lucky to be alive? why didn't they get better.

The button next to the trigger on a pistol is the magazine release, whereas on a shotgun, it's typically the safety. Also, whenever you work the action (like pulling the bolt), it ejects the round in the chamber. Last time I did that, I lost a buckshot - pretty unfortunate.

drinkable water here is really scarce, and i dont think that tar-looking stuff is safe. some of the vegetation here has enough water content to be crushed into a juice, although the taste isnt very pleasant. it beats being thirsty though

i steped in another one of theose damned coils again today, it was hidden in a bush, I noticed it too late to pull back.
<>
it was horrible, like i was being cooked from the inside, even after the initial shock. cold drinks seem to help though, i had to drink most of my rationed milk, and some apple juice, not sure if my brain would have survived without it. evrything is still spinning, could barely even make it back to my pod.

I do not have any water left in my bottle in this desert. I was very dirty and thirsty. I stepped in a puddle I found to drink it and take a bath, then all the dirt made it cloudy. I couldnt drink it anymore. I should have tossed my bottle in. I need water...
dune see big monster. motivated to kill      dune chase monster and kill and eat . great food . great hunter of beast !

WILL FIND AGAIN

i... i felt myself dying. it was all so sudden, there was blood everywhere and i couldnt keep my eyes open. i was scared... but i didnt want to give up. i found so many cool things in this place and i feel like ive made so much progress in this mission. im not gonna stop here. i will make it through all of this shit. i will see my friends again.

i didnt think that monster would find me again, but it did. it knew my face, my mistakes... but not my rifle. when it finally burst open, i saw that pickaxe. i saw the etching i scratched into it, the same as the etching they scratched into my pickaxe. i saw the chip from when they tried to dig up some titanium to gift me. and then, suddenly, i saw it happening again. the smell of grating steel. the tremours of the ground. the sound of dismembering flesh. the taste of sweat and blood, as i tried to run, dropping the food i was bringing back to the pod-
<>
am i supposed to feel at ease now? or am i just bargaining for peace, to be broken when another finds me?

I want to leave this place, I am terrorized, I don't have words to describe what I just experienced, it was a big, horrid creature with hundreds of teeth, ready to devour me, it chase after me for what I've felt hours, it destroys everything on its path. It stopped only after I've went so far away that I was completely lost, it drained my food, water, tools, even my own sanity, I just want this to end.

way too cold in here. have to try my best to not get wet at all, or ill be in big trouble. lucky us, there are a lot of those weird sporey plants that give off some heat, along with those weird machines. run between those when possible, i guess. better not get stuck in the freezing cold.
dune cold. dig warm not warm enough. dune eat fur friend. eat raw! dune has fur. fur warm for dune. dune full and warm. dune happy not cold.

its cold down here, i keep having to duck into lifepods to warm up… although sometimes theres these little humming stoves that i can stop by for warmth, i just need to be careful not to touch them for too long, they make me feel sick. just enough to warm up and then keep going. if i were you id play it safe and look out for anything digging towards me when im resting. i can hear stuff tunneling around me.

It's odd how it rains here. I've never actually seen real rain before... It's rather pretty, even if unsanitary. I still wonder how it is possible for there to be such a higher water concentration down here. Perhaps, the water from the above ice cap is dripping down to here?
found some weird bouncey things. they sent me flying... but i could keep my feet pointed down. its pretty fun. maybe could be useful sometimes, too. kind of dangerous maybe, but what isnt down here.

I dont know what hit me but I cant feel my leg anymore, all I can see is a tangled mess of muscle bone and blood
my body is screaming for the agony to end 
the only thing I have is this blue device on my chest. I was told by the trader I bought it from that it should only be used in emergencies.
The pain is not unbearable anymore, but the light is blinking now. I dont know how long I have left. will the darkness return when this thing runs out? I dont want to fade away...

every single one of these growths here seems like it has some kind of effect... i dont know what exactly. its unpredictable. but there are patterns to it. some are safe, some arent, some are helpful. i'll try to write things down as i figure it out...

..ugh. okay. the yellow ones HURT. feels kind of similar to the coils i found a few hours back... they usually have some kind of weird zappy field around them... don't touch them. its not good. for your body, at least. i noticed it did somehow recharge all my batteries after touching...
this geiger counter i have goes crazy when around one of the green growths. some kind of "radiation", whatever that is. im pretty sure its bad for you. avoid being near those if you can.

noticed some of these are weirdly fragile. they shatter when i touch them at all. youll see they look really brittle, there's cracks all over them. weirdly enough, this seems to also appear alongside some of the other types ive seen... wonder if this is the case for all of them.
noticed a lot of the growths either push me away or pull me in. you can feel some kind of force going through you when that happens. could prove useful sometimes

gosh. i am SO glad i had an ice pack on me. it seems like the foggy, shimmery ones rapidly heat things up around them... including me. i wouldnt be surprised if some did the opposite. i heard this weird sound coming from them too...

make sure you have some antibiotics n such. i think the purple ones mess with your immune system. they have a vague purple haze around them... i sat next to one for a while to rest, and noticed my vital signs dropping on my chip. seems like some kind of sepsis response. i think i'll be fine

okay. the shakey ones look really scary... they aren't. they send you flying... but, like, i can keep my feet pointed down the entire time. kind of similar to the bouncey things in the previous area i was in.

...
<>...
<>...
<>the flying things. i dont know how they did that. holy... oh god. im fine now, but...<>

how to say this... theres weird sharp flying things. they make really weird loud noises... almost as if theyre laughign at me. they wind up sometimes and charge at me, trying to stab me. they got me once or twice, but... whenever they did that, they got stuck in the ground and i hit it a few times until it... uh... not sure... what happened, but it was gone. i think it came from one of the growths, but it was kind of moving.<>

i got something after it disappeared... maybe its useful? it feels like its growing into something, ill wait and see.

okay. good news and bad news. the thing i kept around after killing one of the flying things... it... grew into. more of the flying things. two of them... from one... they seemed to be slower in a group though... like, they were... giving me a chance to fight back? weird.<>

i ate the cores i got from them, i didnt have any other ideas for what to do with it... it was. pretty good, actually. and it seems like it didnt morph into anything this time. maybe i could do this every once in a while to get more and more food? i cant exactly find much of it down here anyway

no clue where i am. i think i touched a weird growth and got... sent. somewhere. safely, though. still, no clue where. all i remember is it looking a bit more see-through than usual. almost as if i could see the place i ended up in...
noticed a lot of these things drip liquids. doesnt seem like they do much else though. water source, maybe?

touched a gray one, it flashed white, made a weird noise. didnt feel much afterwards... until i tried using my laser drill. thing was out of power, when i was sure it wasnt a moment ago. checked the rest of the batteries i saved up, same story. weird. avoid. might be worth throwing myself at one of the zappy ones to recharge them...
eyes hurt. next to a super super bright one. doesnt seem like its doing anything other than... well.. blinding me. dont like it, but i prefer that over whatever other things they can do.
the reddish ones blow up. run when it ticks down. i'm fine... god, i sure as hell almost wasn't.

found a weird hazy one. touched it, and i couldnt see for a bit... but when i opened my eyes, i saw some rope and a bag laying infront of me. the growth was gone. i cant complain. doesnt seem like it did anything else.

you can touch the green ones. theyre good. i liked that a lot. hehe. uh. the not geiger counter activating green ones. the good ones seem like they bounce up a bit. oh... uh... huh... i liked the green one a lot. i like the green one. you can touch it. i like the green one.
found a burning one. touched it out of curiosity. don't do that. it was hot.
huh. i was walking on one of the growth and it looked normal, but it suddenly made a sound and became different. like. it switched places? weird.

This memorandum establishes mandatory terminology policy regarding the Sawian species and supersedes all prior documentation, annotations, and informal usage.

Effective immediately, the term "hermaphrodite" is **banned in all corporate communications**, including scientific, operational, cultural, and casual contexts. The sole exception is explicit historical reference (e.g., quotations, legacy documentation analysis, or discussion of obsolete Earth taxonomies), which must be clearly labeled as such.

The approved descriptor for Sawian reproductive biology is "gonosimulistic."  
Context-dependent alternatives include "cosexual" and "monoecious."

Use of the term outside of these narrow historical contexts is considered inappropriate, reductive, and professionally suspect. This introduces avoidable interpretive risk and will be reviewed under the Stakeholder-Facing Language Control Framework.
<>
No human naturally possesses two fully functional reproductive systems. The appropriate human medical and social term is "intersex", not "hermaphrodite". The latter term originated from the mythological figure Hermaphroditus and has long been associated with stigma, violence, and dehumanization. This includes the use of this term to refer to animals, thus equating intersex humans with animals. This has thus proven volatile in public, academic, and media-facing environments.
<>
The application of this term to non-human species arose from that same framework and is therefore neither neutral nor acceptable, regardless of biological differences.
<>
While historical alternatives exist, such as "eunuch", "bisexual", and "androgyny", these have taken on new meanings and connotations and are not appropriate to use in this way either.

Do not project human gender or sex frameworks onto Sawian subjects. Reproductive biology does not imply gender identity, presentation, orientation, or social function.

Reproductive terminology is not casual vocabulary and should not be treated as such.
<>
This policy does not constitute a moral position, medical guidance, or cultural endorsement. Its purpose is strictly to ensure consistency, reduce ambiguity, and protect the organization from reputational exposure.

[ATTACHMENTS END]

[BIOMETRIC REPORT - 25##-08-04-09-05-25-1057]
[Project: "A day in the life"]
----------------------------------------------<>
So.
This is my "journal" now. Which is apparently a written work describing one's life and experiences.
I am not entirely sure why the coats have me doing this, but the same can be said about most things they make us do.
Still, I got lucky today. It can get way worse than a writing exercise.
I find this device quite fascinating, and it is a shame that I will part with it in just a few hours. But I have been given a task, and I shall complete it.<>

We usually are woken up either by eachother, or by the first food buzzer ringing. Rarely do we wake up to testing immediately.
I'm not sure what "hour" this happens at... Hell, I barely understand the concept. Though, I hear the coats talking about it all the time...

Anyway, we wake up and soon it's time for our very first meal of the day.
What they feed us is slightly varied, which wasn't the case just a few years ago. Still mostly just nutrient bars, though. They're really bland, but I can't complain...

--------------------------------------------------<>
It's some hours later now.

This is the time when the coats hunt.
They call you out by the number, and you have to come to them.
Or they come for you. 
You do not want that.
Sometimes, they call out multiple numbers... It's usually a good sign, though.<>

What they'll do to you is up to chance, really.
The tests can be harmless, they can be terrible, or they can be beneficial.
It's worth noting that the coats merely TRY to do things, and while sometimes they try to help, those attempts are usually far less successful than the ones where they try to mess you up.<>

Two numbers are called, and two others stand up, their faces worried.
One of them is healthy. Far more likely to get hurt.

The other has four arms.
One on the left, and three on the right.
Well, if you could call the weird growths arms. They look kinda close?
They were originally born with two. The coats decided that to be too much one day, and removed one.
The next day, they came back with ELEVEN arms.
The humans removed ten of those, only to grace them with seven new ones.
The next day it was five.
Today it's four.

They are getting close now.<>

The poor fellow is called Arms. And I swear, this was their name BEFORE this whole ordeal, and it's widely believed that humans picked them because of that.
Perhaps it's their idea of a joke?

[PAGE 1 OF 2]

[BIOMETRIC REPORT - 25##-08-04-09-05-25-1057]
[Project: "A day in the life"]
----------------------------------------------
<>
It's quiet. Those who get lucky often just rest; sometimes they socialize. Usually, they just worry about those who got taken.
This concept of "Hours", this cycle, schedule... It's terrifying.
I don't understand it. Why is each sleep cycle divided into 24 (?) hours? What governs such rules? Even the humans play by it.
Is something above them controlling it?
<>

Some more hours later, the first test subjects start to trickle back in.
Best-case scenario, they walk in on their own. Quite common, actually.
Sometimes they are carried in.
Rarely, two guards come in instead. They head straight to someone's cot and take away their things.
If it were your friend, and the guards are some of the nicer ones, they will let you keep a thing or two.
I don't really have anyone to wait for anymore, so I just watch those who do. Everyone is used to this, but the nerves are always present.
<>

Dinner time.
The meal dispensers dropped some cooked meat on my plate. Lucky. Better than breakfast, for sure.
Some others hum approvingly, as they get similarly lucky. Others grumble as they get served the same old nutrient bars. It's really like they are feeding us with whatever they have on hand... Maybe this is a test, too?
Sometimes fights break out over the food, but that's rare. The guards are not happy anytime this happens.
 
People talk. They play. They sing.
Some nurse their wounds, or help others nurse theirs.
It's a calm time.
The coats are done for the time being.

Later.
In about an "hour", our last meal of the day will be served.
Bedtime soon after that.
There is not much more to say, really.

Actually, there is one thing. Very rarely, extra tests will be called around this time. Well, the humans call them "Relocations" or "Assignments."
<>
You never come back when you get called to one of those. But they always allow you to take your things. I am not sure if it's because they are really taking you somewhere else, or they simply want to expedite the process of emptying your place by having you do it yourself.
